assessment_items.question_content:
**What important Cuban agricultural crop is highlighted in *The Jungle*? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad838862cae253699ef768a8fea7647f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: One part of the flora in this scene—sugarcane—is alien to the jungle setting suggested by the painting’s title.

In the 1940s, sugar production was a major part of the Cuban economy.  

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/global-culture/art-between-wars/latin-american-modernism1/a/lam-the-jungle)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What artistic influence is suggested by the jumbled forms and distorted proportions of Lam's figures?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad838862cae253699ef768a8fea7647f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Santería is an Afro-Cuban religion that mixes African beliefs and customs with Catholicism.

The formal distortions of Lam's figures reflect the artistic influence of Modernist painting styles such as Cubism and Surrealism.  

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/global-culture/art-between-wars/latin-american-modernism1/a/lam-the-jungle)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is Wifredo Lam's*The Jungle* significant in the history of Cuban art?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Lam's work created a new narrative within the Cuban imagination: rooted in the island’s complex history, his work was an antidote to the stereotypes (see below) used to promote the Cuban tourism industry.  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/396c92015edcc364ec48930d1ed624bb.jpg)

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/global-culture/art-between-wars/latin-american-modernism1/a/lam-the-jungle)**

choices.content: It rejected stereotypical images of Cuba.
It helped bolster tourism to Cuba.
It drew on the rich cultural history of Cuba.
It promoted Cuban liberation from Spain.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do traditional practices of Santería reflect Surrealist ideas in art?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Santería is an Afro-Cuban religion that mixes African beliefs and customs with Catholicism.

Similar to the Surrealist interest in opening the mind to surreality, Santería ceremonies are moments of metamorphosis where a being is at once itself and otherworldly. 

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/global-culture/art-between-wars/latin-american-modernism1/a/lam-the-jungle)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which issue is NOT addressed in Wifredo Lam's *The Jungle*? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad838862cae253699ef768a8fea7647f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Begun in 1953, the Cuban Revolution ended in 1959 when troops led by Fidel Castro  overthrew the government of Fulgencio Batista.

Set in the lush natural landscape of Cuba, *The Jungle* reflects  Lam's interest in his country's socio-economic conditions and rich cultural heritage.  

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/global-culture/art-between-wars/latin-american-modernism1/a/lam-the-jungle)**

choices.content: the natural beauty of the Cuban landscape
the economic reality of Cuban workers
the mixed cultural heritage of Cuba
the violence of the Cuban Revolution
